company.name: ZOLLERN GmbH & Co. KG
company.legalForm: GmbH & Co. KG (limited partnership with a limited liability company as general partner)
company.ownership: Family-owned (50% Karl Friedrich von Hohenzollern, 50% Merckle Unternehmensgruppe since 1989)

ceo.name: Dr. Jerry Mackel, Dr. Oliver Picht, Mario Zirn
ceo.note: Three-person Geschaeftsfuehrung (Managing Directors); Karl Friedrich Prince of Hohenzollern chairs the Advisory Board (Beiratsvorsitzender)

revenue.note: Industry estimate — ZOLLERN is private with no public filings. Single-source figure.

employees.note: Approximately 2,300 group-wide per direct briefing; ~800 in Steel Profiles division (estimated). Wikipedia cites ~3,200 (likely peak); Craft.co shows ~1,000 (entity scope mismatch).

sites: Additional offices and plants per company website
Additional offices per company website — 17 plants and offices total across Europe, Asia, and North America

divisions.name: Steel Profiles (Stahlprofile)
divisions.description: Custom-geometry precision steel profiles via hot rolling, cold drawing, cold rolling, and optional induction hardening. Serves 12+ application sectors including linear guides, automotive, elevator rails, firearms, and industrial machinery.

divisions.name: Drive Technology (Antriebstechnik)
divisions.description: Gearboxes, winches, direct drives, automation systems, and rotary table systems for industrial applications.

divisions.name: Investment Casting (Feinguss)
divisions.description: Investment casting for aerospace, industrial, and energy applications. Growth segment.

divisions.name: Sand Casting & Forging (Sandguss und Schmiede)
divisions.description: Sand casting, shell mould casting, centrifugal casting, and forging for heavy industrial applications.

divisions.name: Bearing Technology (Gleitlagertechnik)
divisions.description: Plain bearings, hydrostatic and aerostatic bearings via subsidiary BHW Gleitlager.

productGroup.name: Special Steel Profiles
productGroup.scope: Custom-geometry precision steel profiles — hot-rolled, cold-drawn, cold-rolled — with optional induction hardening and mechanical processing

families.name: Hot-Rolled Profiles
families.technology: Hot rolling through custom roll sets

families.name: Cold-Drawn Profiles
families.technology: Cold drawing through carbide die sets
families.status: Core production — cross-sections 5-2,500 mm2, IT8 tolerance

families.name: Induction-Hardened Profiles
families.technology: Selective induction surface hardening up to 64 HRC

variants.note: Bulk shaping from billet via custom roll sets
Precision finishing through carbide dies — primary value driver
Selective surface hardening up to 64 HRC, depth 0.5-5 mm

q1.german: Welche Anwendungen und Maerkte fuer Stahlprofile gibt es, wie attraktiv sind diese Markt-Segmente und wie koennte ein passendes Geschaeftsmodell aussehen?
q1.english: What applications and markets exist for steel profiles, how attractive are these market segments, and what could a fitting business model look like?
q1.answer: Analysis ongoing. The study evaluates both adjacent opportunities in 12 existing application markets (linear guides, firearms, pump components, rail vehicles, door systems, elevator guide rails, automotive, generator keybars, hydraulic cylinders, construction machinery, medical instruments, general mechanical engineering) and new market discovery via NAICS/UNSPSC classification. The near-net-shape value proposition — eliminating 40-80% of downstream machining waste — is the central differentiator. Markets must absorb meaningful tonnage to justify idle capacity utilization.

q2.german: Wo koennen angrenzende Produkt- und Marktchancen die freie Kapazitaet absorbieren und den Margen-Mix verbessern?
q2.english: Where can adjacent product and market opportunities absorb idle capacity and improve the margin mix?
q2.answer: Analysis ongoing. The strategic imperative is to offset revenue erosion in traditional core markets under intense price competition. With an estimated 30-40% idle capacity across key processes, the study identifies both upward movement in the value network (from semi-finished material to finished components) and lateral expansion into high-value application sectors where precision geometry, surface hardness, and near-net-shape economics create defensible margins.
